major_news_summary: Stocks closed sharply higher as investor sentiment improved amid a pullback in oil prices after President Trump cancelled strikes against Iran, claiming a deal had been agreed in principle by several allies in the Middle East. U.S. wholesale prices surged in May — headline PPI climbed 1.1% vs. conse

US stocks surged on Monday, with tech leading the gains, while oil prices sank as investors welcomed a breakthrough US-Iran deal to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.9% to hit a fresh record.

The Dow Jones (+0.74%) managed to ascend to a new record, while the Nasdaq (–0.81%), Russell 2000 (–0.63%), and S&P 500 (–0.40%) hit day lows as a downturn in tech stocks steepened into the afternoon. The 10-year Treasury yield held around 4.47% as investors shifted focus from the US-Iran peace deal

Declines were owed to commentary after the FOMC meeting, which revealed an increasing bias among Fed members to raise rates later this year. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh did little to settle those projections, even though he said none of the Fed participants felt the need to raise rates today. Policyma
